title: Today's Markets Brief: These stocks fell the most Friday as Big Tech took a dive
summary: A quick rundown of the market's biggest losers and growing scrutiny on prediction market firms.
imageAlt: Image from source article for These stocks fell the most Friday as Big Tech took a dive
imageCaption: These stocks fell the most Friday as Big Tech took a dive
imageSourceTitle: These stocks fell the most Friday as Big Tech took a dive

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your weekend markets brief. I am looking at the biggest stock movers from Friday, and it's clear that Big Tech took a bit of a dive. Chip makers dominated the list of the day's biggest losers among the S&P 500. It's always interesting to see which stocks get hit the hardest, and what that might mean for the broader market. I mean, who doesn't love a good stock drama?

According to MarketWatch, these stocks fell the most on Friday as Big Tech took a dive. I won't bore you with the entire list, but it's worth noting that chip makers were a big part of the decline. My read is that this could be a sign that investors are getting a little nervous about the sector's earnings expectations. You know how it is - high expectations can be a double-edged sword.

Also, prediction market firms are facing growing scrutiny from Congress. A bill proposed by Rep. Bryan Steil aims to ban Congress members and their staff from certain bets on prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket. What stands out to me here is that this could have implications for the regulation of these firms and potentially impact their valuation. It's fascinating to see how lawmakers are trying to keep up with the rapidly evolving world of prediction markets.

My quick read: today's market story has that familiar split-screen feeling. There is real excitement around artificial intelligence and chips, but the room still keeps one eye on risk, because markets enjoy a celebration more when they know where the exits are.
